title: Why Is The Crypto Market Up Today?
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The crypto market is up today due to pre-FOMC positioning, with traders shifting from equities to cryptos as the Fed is expected to hold rates steady. The marke
excerpt: The crypto market is up today due to pre-FOMC positioning, with traders shifting from equities to cryptos as the Fed is expected to hold rates steady. The market cap rose 1.11% to $2.54 trillion, reclaiming key support levels.

content.introduction: The crypto market is up today due to pre-FOMC positioning, with traders shifting from equities to cryptos as the Fed is expected to hold rates steady. The market cap rose 1.11% to $2.54 trillion, reclaiming key support levels. The crypto market is up 1.11% to $2.54 trillion as pre-FOMC de-risking pulls capital out of S&P 500 into crypto ahead of Powell's decision.
body:
## Current Market Dynamics

The broader cryptocurrency market shows a mild recovery on Friday, with Bitcoin rising above $77,000, up from Wednesday's low of $74,937. Altcoins, including The overall crypto market is growing this week. As of today, the total crypto market capitalization is 2.53 trillion, representing a 0.20% increase from The crypto market is up today due to pre-FOMC positioning, with traders shifting from equities to cryptos as the Fed is expected to hold rates steady. The market cap rose 1.11% to $2.54 trillion, reclaiming key support levels. The total crypto market cap rose 1.11% from yesterday's lows to $2.54 trillion, reclaiming the 0.236 Fibonacci level at $2.54 trillion. The level has held as the most important support since rebounding from the March 29 low at $2.23 trillion. The S&P 500 closed yesterday's session down nearly 0.5%, while crypto held flat-to-positive. With the Fed widely expected to hold rates at 3.50% to 3.75%, traders are possibly de-risking from equities and parking capital in crypto ahead of Powell's press conference. CenterPoint Securities CenterPoint Securities How Crypto Markets May Impact Stock Markets The Relationship Between Cryptos & Stocks Cryptocurrency markets have finally become mainstream. Cable financial news network tickers include popular crypto prices in addition to the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq benchmark index prices. Crypto ETFs, futures and options are increasing in liquidity and popularity. Most importantly, financial institutions and bell weather companies are not only acknowledging but investing in cryptos. This sparks the inevitable question of how crypto markets impact the stock markets. Crypto trading may serve as a gateway to stock trading (and vice versa). The increase in participants in one market may lead to an increase in participants in the other. Hot Sectors Themes When crypto markets are hot, certain stocks, sectors, and themes may also be hot. There are certain stocks that are highly correlated to the crypto markets. Examples include: Any wide swings or trends in the crypto markets will impact the price moves in crypto-themed stocks that have holdings or operate in the crypto markets. For example, if bitcoin is breaking out, the stock of a company that mines bitcoin may break out as well. Crypto ETFs and Stocks Crypto ETFs and Stocks ... Stocks and cryptos move based on supply and demand (i.e. However, stocks have fundamental and quantitative-based intrinsic valuations based on the underlying company performance metrics. Cryptos in general have no intrinsic value since they don t represent underlying companies with quarterly earnings reports to gauge the performance of the business operations. In that sense, it could be argued that cryptos are a more pure supply and demand driven market.

## Key Drivers and Catalysts

How Crypto Markets Affect Stock Markets Since crypto markets are relatively new compared to stock markets, the question arises as to the level of correlation that exists between stock and crypto markets. Are the crypto markets coupled or linked to the performance of the stock markets? Until recently, 2025 was a banner year for crypto entering the economic mainstream. Crypto exchange Coinbase joined the S&P 500, Congress passed a law allowing major banks to play with stablecoins, and even crypto holdout JP Morgan announced it would allow clients to use Bitcoin as collateral for loans. All of those connections between the real economy and the blockchain were happening mostly when crypto was on the upswing. Longtime crypto investors, like Leigh Drogan at Starkiller Capital, are used to watching crypto prices fall off a cliff. But he said this most recent nosedive was different, because crypto had reached the top of the cliff just a second ago. Marketplace Facebook Instagram YouTube TikTok Bluesky Volume Button Volume Button Backward 15 seconds Forward 15 seconds Crypto went mainstream. So what happens now that its value is tanking? government and banks have legitimized cryptocurrencies, a huge drop in value could have real-world consequences. A crypto downturn could have knock-on effects in the greater economy. Bitcoin has taken a bit of a beating recently. Since reaching an all-time high in early October, the OG cryptocurrency has lost about a quarter of it s value and the overall crypto market is down about a trillion dollars. Even though prices might be down now, crypto enthusiasts say there will be more opportunities for everyday investors to get in on the action. I think it s gonna be a crypto-ETF-palooza over the next year, said Matt Hougan at Bitwise Asset Management. ETF is short for exchange traded fund basically an easy way for investors not fluent in the blockchain to put their money in crypto. Related Topics Collections: Tagged as: Our best stories, straight to your inbox Sign up for the Marketplace newsletter to get stories that help you live smarter plus analysis, explainers and our song of the week sent to you every Friday morning. Latest Stories Stories You Might Like Accessibility. Crypto is relatively new (bitcoin was created in 2009) and new investment options are gradually coming to market. When new vehicles like spot crypto ETPs (including the spot bitcoin ETP and spot ether ETP) go live, their inflows and outflows can impact cryptocurrencies prices. Spot crypto ETPs can be particularly impactful because they hold actual bitcoin as their underlying asset. Every 4 years, the Bitcoin network is programmed to cut the amount of mining rewards in half. The goal is to stabilize bitcoin s ability to act as a store of value. Meanwhile, the Ethereum network periodically receives upgrades that seek to improve the way it operates.

## Outlook and Risk Factors

Events like these can positively or negatively affect the cryptocurrency s price. Crypto is a high-risk investment compared with most traditional investments (e.g., stocks and bonds). Like other riskier investments, the price of crypto may have a tendency to perform better when the economy and or market is generally thought of as doing well or expected to do well, and investors are willing to take more risk. Alternatively, price may go down when the economy or market is not performing well or is not expected to perform well, and investors are generally not as willing to invest in riskier assets. A significant source of uncertainty for bitcoin and other crypto prices is government regulation, along with any other actions that governments may take in regard to cryptocurrencies. Domestically, crypto supporters have been lobbying for more defined regulations for the crypto industry as a whole. News or rumors of potential regulations has been known to impact price. Similar to how geopolitical and macroeconomic news can affect stocks and other investments, they can also influence the price of crypto over the short and long term. For example, overseas wars can push investors to reduce the amount of risk they re willing to take, which can negatively impact the price of bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies. 13 Aral k 2024, 15:47 Yeni Halk Ekmek B fesi Efe Meydan 'nda Hizmete Giriyor 31 Ocak 2025, 10:36 Beylerbeyi Yeni Mahalle de Yol ilesi Sona Eriyor!
